title: Compliance Status Trending

compliance.assurance_rationale: Monitors device configuration compliance status against Catalyst Center policies, providing evidence of configuration baseline enforcement.
compliance.controlObjective: Shows that compliant versus non-compliant device counts and compliance percentage are tracked over time (CM-6), not just at a point in time.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Report `UC-5.13.30` (daily timechart) stored in the compliance dashboard; optional email digest of the compliance_pct series for audit folders.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Demonstrates network device configuration compliance monitoring as part of firewall and router configuration standards.
compliance.controlObjective: Gives a running history that configuration standards (1.1.1) are not only defined but also monitored for sustained conformance.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Same dashboard panel plus monthly CSV export of the timechart to evidence storage with retention that matches your PCI assessment cycle.

dataSources: `index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:compliance` from the `compliance` modular input in `TA_cisco_catalyst` (Splunkbase 7538). Same data feed as UC-5.13.28. For trending, retain **90–365 days** of compliance data to demonstrate sustained compliance to auditors. Volume: ~400 bytes/device/poll × 24 polls/day.
spl:
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:compliance"
| where complianceStatus IN ("COMPLIANT","NON_COMPLIANT")
| timechart span=1d count(eval(complianceStatus="COMPLIANT")) as compliant count(eval(complianceStatus="NON_COMPLIANT")) as non_compliant
| eval compliance_pct=if((compliant+non_compliant)>0, round(compliant*100/(compliant+non_compliant),1), null())
description: Tracks the compliance posture over time as a stacked area chart and compliance percentage line, demonstrating to auditors that configuration standards are continuously monitored and that violations are remediated promptly — not just at point-in-time assessment snapshots.
value: Auditors ask: 'How do you know your devices were compliant *between* assessments?' This UC answers that question with a continuous daily trend line. A compliance percentage that holds at 95%+ for 90 days is the strongest evidence for NIST CM-6 and PCI 1.1.1 that your configuration baseline is enforced as an ongoing process. Dips in the trend correspond to template pushes, firmware campaigns, or drift events — and the recovery slope proves your remediation process works. Without this trend, your compliance narrative is 'we checked on audit day and it was fine' — with it, you show 365 days of continuous enforcement.
implementation: Same `compliance` input as UC-5.13.28. Retain 90–365 days for audit evidence. The `timechart span=1d` produces daily compliance snapshots. Schedule daily CSV export for the GRC evidence folder. Handle zero-denominator days with `if((compliant+non_compliant)>0, ..., null())`.
visualization: (1) Stacked area chart: `compliant` (green) vs `non_compliant` (red) over time — the visual narrative of compliance posture. (2) Line overlay: `compliance_pct` on a secondary y-axis (0–100%) with an SLO reference line at 95%. (3) Stat panel: current compliance_pct, 30-day average, 90-day average. (4) Annotations: overlay template change dates, maintenance windows, and audit dates for assessor context.

references.title: Catalyst Center Intent API — Compliance endpoint
NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5 — CM-6 Configuration Settings

knownFalsePositives:
**Golden template update creating a step change in the compliance trend.** When the golden template is updated, the ratio of COMPLIANT to NON_COMPLIANT devices may shift dramatically on the day of the update. Distinguish by checking whether the trend shows a sharp, one-time shift corresponding to a template change. Suppress by annotating the trend chart with template change dates from audit logs.

**Compliance re-scan cadence affecting trend smoothness.** The compliance API polls hourly by default. Changes in compliance status may appear as step functions rather than smooth trends. `span=1d` smooths this into daily snapshots. No suppression needed.

**Device batch onboarding creating a temporary dip in the compliance ratio.** When many new devices are added to Catalyst Center, they may initially show as NON_COMPLIANT until configurations are pushed. Distinguish by checking whether device count increased in the same period. Suppress by tracking new devices separately and allowing a 48-hour onboarding grace period.

**Seasonal compliance audit preparation changing compliance behaviour.** Before a compliance audit, teams may run additional compliance checks or update templates, creating temporary turbulence in the trend. Distinguish by correlating with audit preparation schedules. No suppression needed — this is expected pre-audit activity.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• UC-5.13.28 (Compliance Status Overview) must be operational — same `compliance` data feed.
• Index retention: set `frozenTimePeriodInSecs` for the `catalyst` index to cover your compliance reporting period. NIST AU-11 recommends retaining audit records per your organisation's retention policy — typically **1–3 years** for CM-6 evidence. PCI DSS 10.7 requires at least **12 months**. Compliance data is ~400 bytes/device/poll, so even 365 days for 2,000 devices is manageable (~2.8 GB).
• Agree with the compliance team on the SLO: what percentage counts as 'compliant'? 95%? 98%? Document this as the reference line on the chart.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Same `compliance` input as UC-5.13.28. No additional configuration.

For the trend to be useful as audit evidence, verify continuous data collection:
```spl
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:compliance" earliest=-90d
| timechart span=1d count
| where count = 0
```
Zero-count days are data gaps that will show as holes in the trend — investigate and document each gap.

Step 2 — Create the search and report
```spl
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:compliance"
| where complianceStatus IN ("COMPLIANT","NON_COMPLIANT")
| timechart span=1d count(eval(complianceStatus="COMPLIANT")) as compliant count(eval(complianceStatus="NON_COMPLIANT")) as non_compliant
| eval compliance_pct=if((compliant+non_compliant)>0, round(compliant*100/(compliant+non_compliant),1), null())
```

Why filter to `IN ("COMPLIANT","NON_COMPLIANT")`: excludes ERROR, IN_PROGRESS, and NOT_APPLICABLE statuses that would inflate the denominator. The compliance percentage should measure only devices that were actually evaluated — not devices whose evaluation failed or wasn't applicable.

Why `if((compliant+non_compliant)>0, ..., null())`: prevents division by zero on days with no compliance data (holidays, TA outages). A null percentage is visually distinct from 0% in the chart — it shows a gap rather than a false 'zero compliance' day.

Why `span=1d`: daily granularity is appropriate for compliance trending — compliance posture changes slowly (days, not hours). Hourly granularity would show within-day noise from poll timing without adding operational value.

For the assessor evidence export:
```spl
<base search above>
| eval date=strftime(_time, "%Y-%m-%d")
| table date, compliant, non_compliant, compliance_pct
| outputcsv compliance_trend_<YYYY-MM>.csv
```

Schedule as Report: daily (cron `0 6 * * *`), output to dashboard. Monthly CSV export for the GRC evidence folder.

Step 3 — Validate
(a) Run the search over the last 30 days. The compliance_pct line should be relatively stable (typically 90–100%). Sudden dips should correspond to known events (template changes, device onboarding).

(b) Compare today's compliance_pct with UC-5.13.28's current snapshot. They should agree within 1–2% (daily average vs point-in-time).

(c) Check for data gaps: `| where isnull(compliance_pct)`. Each gap should be documented with a root cause (TA outage, maintenance, etc.) for the assessor narrative.

(d) Verify the SLO reference line: the compliance_pct should be above the agreed SLO (e.g., 95%) on most days. Days below the SLO are potential audit findings that need documentation.

(e) Cross-reference dips with UC-5.13.29 (Non-Compliant Device Alerting) — every dip should have a corresponding alert and remediation record.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard placement (on the "Compliance Posture" dashboard, below UC-5.13.28's donut chart):
• Full-width stacked area chart: compliant (green) vs non_compliant (red) with compliance_pct line overlay.
• SLO reference line at 95% (or your agreed target).
• Time-picker presets: "Last 30 days" (monthly review), "Last 90 days" (quarterly audit), "Last 365 days" (annual assessment).
• Annotations: template change dates, maintenance windows, audit dates.

Compliance evidence workflow:
• **Daily**: scheduled search runs at 6 AM, updates dashboard.
• **Monthly**: CSV export to GRC evidence folder with date-stamped filename.
• **Pre-audit**: generate the 90-day or 365-day trend PDF and include in the evidence pack for CM-6 / PCI 1.1.1.
• **During audit**: give assessors read-only dashboard access via a `compliance_auditor` Splunk role.

Runbook (owner: Compliance team):
1. Review the daily trend. If compliance_pct dropped below the SLO yesterday, investigate with UC-5.13.29 (which devices became non-compliant).
2. For planned dips (template updates): document the change ID, expected duration, and recovery timeline in the compliance narrative.
3. For unplanned dips: escalate to change management. Document the root cause, remediation steps, and prevention plan.
4. Track compliance_pct month-over-month. A declining trend (even above the SLO) indicates growing technical debt.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting

• **Compliance_pct is always 100%** — either all devices are genuinely compliant (verify in Catalyst Center), or the `complianceStatus` field values don't match the filter. Check `| stats values(complianceStatus)`.

• **Large dip that doesn't recover** — a golden template change made many devices permanently non-compliant. The template may need to be pushed, or the template itself may need revision.

• **Data gaps (null compliance_pct days)** — TA outage or compliance input was disabled. Document each gap with root cause for auditors. Re-enable the input and verify recovery.

• **Compliance_pct oscillates day-to-day** — devices are flipping between COMPLIANT and NON_COMPLIANT due to an unstable golden template or intermittent compliance check failures. Investigate with `| stats count by complianceStatus, deviceName | where count > 50` to find the oscillating devices.

• **Denominator is too small** — only a few devices have COMPLIANT/NON_COMPLIANT status, while most are NOT_APPLICABLE. This happens when golden templates are assigned to only a subset of device families. Expand template assignment in Catalyst Center for comprehensive compliance coverage.

• **Trend shows improvement that the assessor disputes** — ensure the search time range matches the assessment period. Show the assessor the raw daily data (table format) alongside the chart.

• **CSV export missing** — the `outputcsv` command requires `schedule_search` capability. Verify the scheduling user's permissions.

• **Chart shows both compliant and non_compliant growing** — device fleet is growing. The stacked area accurately shows the increasing population. Use compliance_pct (the line) as the primary metric, not absolute counts.
grandmaExplanation: We draw a simple day-by-day picture of how many network devices pass or fail their configuration checks, so you can show auditors that standards were held steady over months — not just on the day of the audit. Dips in the chart are explained and fixed, proving your team stays on top of things continuously.
